# drea: podcast ad blocker

podcasts, without the ads

Only for iPhone

Free · Designed for iPhone. Not verified for macOS.

 Share

*   ![Image 5](https://apps.apple.com/assets/artwork/1x1.gif)

*   ![Image 8](https://apps.apple.com/assets/artwork/1x1.gif)    
*   ![Image 9](https://apps.apple.com/assets/artwork/1x1.gif)    

iPhone

Say goodbye to podcast ads. Enjoy your favorite podcasts, ad-free. Trusted by 100% of Zen Buddhist monks. drea is a simple, elegant podcast app that can look inside episodes, figure out where the ads are, and then skip them automatically as you listen. No user account required, and no paid subscription either (yes, you read that right, it's free). Features: • Listen to your favorite podcasts, ad-free • Add shows to your library for easy access • Search for any podcast • Browse top charts by category • Download episodes for offline listening • Bookmark episodes to come back to later • Search for episodes within a podcast • Add podcasts through an RSS feed URL Note: ad skipping is currently only supported for English language podcasts.

more

## [Ratings & Reviews](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/6759070798?see-all=reviews&platform=iphone)

*   4.8 out of 5  12 Ratings                                        

*   
### Great App! One small issue 

4d ago

HCPWJi

Really liking the app so far — clean design and smooth experience, especially for a new release. One small issue I noticed: the forward/back controls on my car’s infotainment system don’t trigger the app’s skip functions. The 30s forward and 15s back buttons work correctly inside the app itself, but not through the car controls, while apps like Pocket Casts handle this properly. Hope this can be added/fixed in a future update!

Developer Response

Will def look into supporting the car controls for back 15 and forward 30. Thank you for sharing that missing functionality. Glad you’re enjoying the app otherwise! Cheers.

# Great App! One small issue

4d ago

HCPWJi

Really liking the app so far — clean design and smooth experience, especially for a new release. One small issue I noticed: the forward/back controls on my car’s infotainment system don’t trigger the app’s skip functions. The 30s forward and 15s back buttons work correctly inside the app itself, but not through the car controls, while apps like Pocket Casts handle this properly. Hope this can be added/fixed in a future update!

Developer Response 2d ago

Will def look into supporting the car controls for back 15 and forward 30. Thank you for sharing that missing functionality. Glad you’re enjoying the app otherwise! Cheers.

*   
### Great Start 

2d ago

crosser98

This is a great baseline for a podcast app, but it’s missing too many features. Auto downloads, auto queue, silence trimming, playlists.Ideally, as a minimum, we should be able to make a queue based on a list of podcasts, and then as they get added and downloaded, the ad skipping could be done then.

Developer Response

Thank you for checking out drea, and for your review!Will gradually try to add the features you listed. Just wanted to ship an initial simple version with the core ad skipping and a basic set of normal podcast app features.

# Great Start

2d ago

crosser98

This is a great baseline for a podcast app, but it’s missing too many features. Auto downloads, auto queue, silence trimming, playlists.Ideally, as a minimum, we should be able to make a queue based on a list of podcasts, and then as they get added and downloaded, the ad skipping could be done then.

Developer Response 1d ago

Thank you for checking out drea, and for your review!Will gradually try to add the features you listed. Just wanted to ship an initial simple version with the core ad skipping and a basic set of normal podcast app features.

*   
### Just works 

3d ago

BrainHurl

I appreciate the simplicity. The ad recognition only takes seconds and works well. Very well done.

# App Privacy

The developer, **Hamza Qayyum**, indicated that the app’s privacy practices may include handling of data as described below. This information has not been verified by Apple. For more information, see the [developer’s privacy policy](https://drea.fm/privacy).
